description: Plain English body type descriptions for lay-persons

A.name: Balanced
A.plainEnglish: You have good energy levels, sleep well, and rarely get sick. Your body handles stress well and recovers quickly. This is the ideal state of health.
quickTips: Keep up your healthy habits
Stay physically active
Eat a varied, balanced diet

B.name: Low Energy
B.plainEnglish: You often feel fatigued, may catch colds easily, and sometimes feel weak or short of breath. You might prefer to avoid strenuous activities.
quickTips: Get plenty of rest and sleep
Eat warm, cooked foods (soups, stews)
Try gentle exercise like walking or tai chi

C.name: Cold Sensitivity
C.plainEnglish: Your hands and feet are often cold, you dislike cold weather, and you prefer warm drinks and foods. You may feel sluggish in winter.
quickTips: Keep warm, especially your feet and lower back
Drink warm beverages throughout the day
Avoid cold and raw foods

D.name: Heat Sensitivity
D.tagline: You run warm and feel dry
D.plainEnglish: You often feel warm, have dry skin or mouth, and may experience night sweats or insomnia. You prefer cool environments and cold drinks.
quickTips: Stay hydrated with water and herbal teas
Eat cooling foods (cucumber, watermelon, pears)
Avoid spicy foods and alcohol

E.name: Sluggish Metabolism
E.tagline: Your body holds onto weight easily
E.plainEnglish: You may gain weight easily, feel heavy or sluggish, have oily skin, or experience bloating. You might feel tired after meals.
quickTips: Eat lighter meals, avoid greasy foods
Stay active with regular cardio exercise
Limit dairy and sweet foods

F.name: Overheated & Oily
F.tagline: You run hot with oily skin
F.plainEnglish: You have oily skin prone to acne, may feel hot and sweaty, and might experience a bitter taste in your mouth. You may feel irritable in hot weather.
quickTips: Avoid fried, greasy, and spicy foods
Eat more vegetables and light foods
Stay cool and keep skin clean

G.name: Poor Circulation
G.tagline: Your blood flow could be better
G.plainEnglish: You may bruise easily, have a darker complexion, or experience pain in fixed locations. Your skin might look dull or have dark spots.
quickTips: Stay active to improve circulation
Eat foods that support blood flow (beets, leafy greens)
Avoid sitting for long periods

H.name: Stress-Prone
H.plainEnglish: You tend to feel anxious or moody, may sigh often, and can feel a tightness in your chest when stressed. Your mood affects your physical wellbeing.
quickTips: Practice stress relief (yoga, meditation, walking)
Talk to friends and stay socially connected
Try calming teas (chamomile, lavender, rose)

I.name: Sensitive & Allergic
I.tagline: Your body reacts easily to triggers
I.plainEnglish: You may have allergies, sensitive skin, or react to certain foods, medications, or environmental factors. You need to be careful about what you're exposed to.
quickTips: Identify and avoid your personal triggers
Keep your environment clean and dust-free
Build immunity with regular sleep and exercise
